title: Corunastylis parvifolia
intro: Corunastylis parvifolia, also known as the Eastern Spinebill Orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family that is native to eastern Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in indigenous medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Corunastylis parvifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Corunastylis parvifolia is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly prepared by herbalists for their potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Corunastylis parvifolia is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Corunastylis, which is a group of terrestrial orchids native to Australia. The species Corunastylis parvifolia is further classified into the subfamily Stereostemmatinae. The plant is also known as the small greenhood due to its small size and green-colored flowers. Its classification into the family Orchidaceae places it among the diverse and widespread orchids found globally.
